MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
Off Calendar Jan-12-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:

Whitney Davis of K.W. DAVIS (916.333.5354), appearing by CourtCall for Defendant / Moving Party Viad Corporation.
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415.898.1555), appearing for Plaintiff Darrell Richardson.

RULING: DEFENDANT VIAD CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES.

Matter called, argument held. After consideration of the arguments of Counsel and the pleadings/papers, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Viad Corp.'s motion for summary judgment and its alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's Negligence I and Products Liability causes of action are denied. As to summary judgment and summary adjudication of plaintiff's products liability cause of action, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant did not attempt to demonstrate that plaintiff does not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence, so defendant may only sustain its initial burden if it conclusively negated an element of plaintiff's cause of action. Defendant failed to do so. Defendant failed to present evidence demonstrating that it is not the successor-in-interest to Griscom-Russell Co. As to summary adjudication of plaintiff's negligence I cause of action, defendant failed to demonstrate that the product line successor rule is the only potential exception that applies, so the discussion in Franklin v. USX Corp. (2001) 87 Cal.App.4th 615 regarding the inapplicability of such exception to ordinary negligence claims is not determinative. Plaintiff's objections to the testimony of Peter Novak are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Court Reporter: Beth E. Grant (INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR), CSR # 10043 (Tel. 707.888.3689). (503/GLW)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Of Crane Co. Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Dec-22-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:

Kimberly Chu, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415.898.1555), appearing for plaintiff / moving party;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

RULING: DEFENDANT CRANE CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

There being no other appearance, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows: Defendant Crane Co.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, clear and convincing evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiff's responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Even if defendant sustained its initial burden, plaintiff's evidence (Exhibits C, E, G, H, N, P and X) creates a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. While the Court takes the higher proof standard into consideration, plaintiff does not need to prove his case for punitive damages at the summary adjudication stage and the Court cannot conclude that no reasonable jury could find plaintiff's evidence to be clear and convincing proof of malice, fraud or oppression. (See American Airlines, Inc. v. Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton (2002) 96 Cal.App.4th 1017; Hoch v. Allied-Signal, Inc. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 48.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Not Reported. =(503/GLW)
